langchain-ai/langgraphjs#545.body:
Hi team, @jacoblee93 @bracesproul Is there a MemorySaver for a database that has a complete or "comprehensive" `.test.ts` written for it?

I've spent the greater part of the last 7 days trying to comprehend what on earth is happening inside the Memory Saver adapter (or whatever this "thing" is called, in order to try creating a custom version that works with SurrealDB. I've been looking at the Sqlite version: "SqliteSaver" and the test file, here: https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraphjs/blob/main/libs/checkpoint-sqlite/src/index.ts#L45
but I'm having errors translating it for SurrealDB. While testing my SdbSaver against the test.ts I'm getting errors here:
```
✓ should get undefined checkpoint
     ✓ should save first checkpoint
     ✓ should add some writes
     × should get first checkpoint tuple
     × should save second checkpoint
     × should list checkpoints
```
In looking at the `.getTuple` method, the Type specified in the Sqlite version, and the corresponding test I'm wondering if the test file is actually correctly written, or if it's incomplete? It could very well be my mistake but I'd greatly appreciate a pointer!

If there is a "complete" reference Saver with an accompanying test file can you please recommend? I realise I'm trying to reverse engineer this, fairly blindly, and now realise the test I'm looking at may have been misleading me.
langchain-ai/langgraphjs#545.title: Custom Checkpointer / MemorySaver - is there a comprehensive saver with a Test file to use as a guide? (LangGraph JS)

liam-hq/liam#2460.body:
## Summary

This issue tracks the removal of ESLint suppressions for the `no-throw-error/no-throw-error` rule in the `@liam-hq/github` package.

## Context

PR #2442 introduced a custom ESLint rule that prohibits `throw new Error()` statements and encourages the use of neverthrow Result types instead. To enable incremental adoption, bulk suppressions were applied to existing violations.

## Task

Replace all `throw new Error()` statements in the `frontend/internal-packages/github/` directory with neverthrow Result types (`err`, `ok`, `ResultAsync`) imported from "neverthrow".

## Files to update

- `frontend/internal-packages/github/eslint-suppressions.json` - Contains 7 suppressed violations

## Steps

1. Review the suppressed violations in `frontend/internal-packages/github/eslint-suppressions.json`
2. Replace `throw new Error()` statements with appropriate neverthrow Result types
3. Import neverthrow types: `import { err, ok, ResultAsync } from "neverthrow"`
4. Update function return types to use `Result<T, E>` or `ResultAsync<T, E>`
5. Update calling code to handle Result types appropriately
6. Run `pnpm lint:eslint --prune-suppressions` to update eslint-suppressions.json after modifications
7. Verify all suppressions are resolved

## Related

- Resolves: #2442
liam-hq/liam#2460.title: Remove no-throw-error ESLint suppressions in @liam-hq/github

liam-hq/liam#2673.body:
## Overview
Update `frontend/apps/app/tsconfig.json` to use the shared base configuration from `@frontend/internal-packages/configs/tsconfig/base.json`.

## Background
Currently, the app application has its own TypeScript configuration and doesn't use the shared strict type constraint settings. This results in:
- Weaker type constraints
- Inconsistent type checking across the project
- Missing benefits from shared configuration

## Current Configuration Status

### `frontend/apps/app/tsconfig.json`
- Defines its own `compilerOptions`
- Has `strict: true` configured but lacks stricter settings
- Does not inherit from base configuration

### `frontend/internal-packages/configs/tsconfig/base.json`
- Strict configuration that extends `@tsconfig/strictest/tsconfig.json`
- Should be used as common configuration across the project

## Proposed Changes

Update the app application's `tsconfig.json` to inherit from base.json:

```json
{
  "extends": "../../internal-packages/configs/tsconfig/base.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    // Only add application-specific settings
  }
}
```

## Expected Benefits
- Consistent strict type checking across the project
- Improved type safety
- Elimination of duplicate configuration and better maintainability
- App application benefits from `@tsconfig/strictest` settings

## Considerations
- New type errors may occur in existing code
- Gradual migration should be considered
liam-hq/liam#2673.title: Use shared TypeScript base config for app tsconfig.json

liam-hq/liam#2679.body:
## Background

In PR #2676, we updated `frontend/apps/app/tsconfig.json` to use a shared base configuration. To avoid extensive codebase changes during the migration, we temporarily disabled the `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` TypeScript compiler option.

## What is `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`?

This TypeScript compiler option adds `undefined` to the type of any property access on objects with index signatures (like arrays, records, etc.).

When enabled:
- Accessing `array[0]` has type `T | undefined` instead of just `T`
- Accessing `record[key]` has type `T | undefined` instead of just `T`

This helps catch potential runtime errors where you access non-existent array elements or object properties.

## Task

Re-enable the `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` option in `frontend/apps/app/tsconfig.json` by:

1. **Audit the codebase** - Find all locations where indexed access is used without undefined checks
2. **Fix violations** - Add proper undefined checks or use safe access patterns:
   - Use optional chaining: `array[0]?.method()`
   - Add explicit checks: `if (array[0] !== undefined) { ... }`
   - Use array methods like `.at()` or `.find()` where appropriate
3. **Remove the override** - Remove `"noUncheckedIndexedAccess": false` from the tsconfig.json compiler options
4. **Verify** - Ensure all TypeScript compilation passes with the option enabled

## References

- Related PR: #2676
- TypeScript Release Notes: [noUncheckedIndexedAccess](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/release-notes/typescript-4-1.html#noucheckedindexedaccess)
liam-hq/liam#2679.title: Re-enable `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` TypeScript strict option in app

liam-hq/liam#2709.body:
# Implement DML-Usecase Mapping for Enhanced Artifact Display

## Background

Currently, the DML generation process creates SQL statements in bulk from use cases, but doesn't maintain the relationship between individual use cases and their corresponding DML queries. This relationship needs to be preserved and persisted for the artifact screen, which should display the DML queries and execution results for each use case.

## Current Implementation Analysis

After thorough investigation of the codebase, I've identified the current state and implementation gaps:

### 1. Use Case Structure (`qaGenerateUsecaseAgent/agent.ts`)

```typescript
const usecaseSchema = v.object({
  requirementType: v.picklist(['functional', 'non_functional']),
  requirementCategory: v.string(),
  requirement: v.string(),
  title: v.string(),
  description: v.string(),
})
```

**Current State:**
- Use cases are generated with category and type information
- No unique identifiers are assigned to individual use cases
- Use cases are stored as `Usecase[]` in `WorkflowState.generatedUsecases`

### 2. DML Generation Process (`prepareDmlNode.ts`)

```typescript
function formatUseCases(useCases: Usecase[]): string {
  // Groups use cases by requirement category
  // Returns formatted string for LLM consumption
}
```

**Current State:**
- `formatUseCases` groups use cases by category but doesn't assign IDs
- `DMLGenerationAgent.generate()` returns only `{ dmlStatements: string }`
- No mapping between individual use cases and their DML statements

### 3. Artifact Schema (`artifact.ts`)

```typescript
export const useCaseSchema = v.object({
  title: v.string(),
  description: v.string(),
  dml_operations: v.array(dmlOperationSchema), // Currently empty
})

export const dmlOperationSchema = v.object({
  operation_type: v.picklist(['INSERT', 'UPDATE', 'DELETE', 'SELECT']),
  sql: v.string(),
  dml_execution_logs: v.array(dmlExecutionLogSchema),
})
```

**Current State:**
- Artifact schema already defines `dml_operations` field for use cases
- `DmlOperation` type includes execution logs for tracking
- Schema is well-designed for the intended functionality

### 4. Transformation Logic (`transformWorkflowStateToArtifact.ts`)

```typescript
const mapUseCasesToRequirements = (usecase: Usecase) => ({
  title: usecase.title,
  description: usecase.description,
  dml_operations: [], // Empty for now - to be populated when DML tracking is added
})
```

**Current State:**
- Transform function explicitly sets `dml_operations` to empty array
- Comment indicates this is a known gap
- Infrastructure exists but mapping logic is missing

## Implementation Approaches

### Approach 1: Modify DMLGenerationAgent Output Format (Recommended)

**Changes Required:**

1. **Update `DMLGenerationAgent` output schema:**
   ```typescript
   const DMLGenerationAgentOutputSchema = v.object({
     usecaseDmlMap: v.record(v.string(), v.array(dmlOperationSchema)),
   })
   ```

2. **Modify `formatUseCases` to assign IDs:**
   ```typescript
   function formatUseCases(useCases: Usecase[]): { 
     formattedText: string; 
     usecaseIdMap: Record<string, Usecase> 
   }
   ```

3. **Update `WorkflowState` type:**
   ```typescript
   export type WorkflowState = {
     // ... existing fields
     usecaseDmlMap?: Record<string, DmlOperation[]>
   }
   ```

4. **Implement mapping in `transformWorkflowStateToArtifact`:**
   ```typescript
   const mapUseCasesToRequirements = (
     usecase: Usecase, 
     usecaseId: string, 
     dmlMap: Record<string, DmlOperation[]>
   ) => ({
     title: usecase.title,
     description: usecase.description,
     dml_operations: dmlMap[usecaseId] || [],
   })
   ```

**Pros:**
- Maintains data integrity from generation to artifact
- Aligns with existing artifact schema
- Enables future DML execution tracking per use case
- Clean separation of concerns

**Cons:**
- Requires LLM prompt engineering to generate structured output
- More complex agent implementation

### Approach 2: Post-Processing DML Statements

**Changes Required:**

1. **Create DML parser to extract operations:**
   ```typescript
   function parseDmlStatements(dmlText: string): DmlOperation[]
   ```

2. **Implement heuristic mapping:**
   ```typescript
   function mapDmlToUsecases(
     dmlOps: DmlOperation[], 
     usecases: Usecase[]
   ): Record<string, DmlOperation[]>
   ```

**Pros:**
- Minimal changes to existing DMLGenerationAgent
- Can work with current string-based output

**Cons:**
- Heuristic mapping may be inaccurate
- Complex parsing logic required
- Fragile to DML format changes

### Approach 3: Hybrid Approach

**Changes Required:**

1. **Enhance DMLGenerationAgent with structured comments:**
   ```sql
   -- USE_CASE_ID: uc_001
   INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES ('John', 'john@example.com');
   ```

2. **Parse comments to extract mappings:**
   ```typescript
   function extractUsecaseMappings(dmlText: string): Record<string, DmlOperation[]>
   ```

**Pros:**
- Gradual migration path
- Maintains human-readable DML
- Easier LLM prompt engineering

**Cons:**
- Relies on comment parsing
- Mixed concerns in SQL output

## Recommended Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Core Infrastructure
1. Add unique ID generation for use cases in `formatUseCases`
2. Update `WorkflowState` type to include `usecaseDmlMap`
3. Modify `DMLGenerationAgent` output schema

### Phase 2: Agent Enhancement
1. Update DML generation prompts to produce structured output
2. Implement structured output parsing in `DMLGenerationAgent.generate()`
3. Update `prepareDmlNode` to store mapping in workflow state

### Phase 3: Artifact Integration
1. Modify `transformWorkflowStateToArtifact` to populate `dml_operations`
2. Update artifact creation/update logic
3. Add validation for DML-usecase consistency

### Phase 4: Execution Tracking (Future)
1. Enhance `validateSchemaNode` to track per-usecase execution
2. Store execution results in `dml_execution_logs`
3. Display execution status in artifact screen

## Files to Modify

1. `frontend/internal-packages/agent/src/chat/workflow/types.ts` - Add `usecaseDmlMap` field
2. `frontend/internal-packages/agent/src/chat/workflow/nodes/prepareDmlNode.ts` - Update DML generation process
3. `frontend/internal-packages/agent/src/langchain/agents/dmlGenerationAgent/agent.ts` - Modify output format
4. `frontend/internal-packages/agent/src/chat/workflow/utils/transformWorkflowStateToArtifact.ts` - Implement mapping
5. `frontend/internal-packages/agent/src/chat/workflow/nodes/validateSchemaNode.ts` - Update execution tracking

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each use case has a unique identifier
- [ ] DML operations are mapped to specific use cases
- [ ] `dml_operations` field in artifacts is populated correctly
- [ ] DML execution results are tracked per use case
- [ ] Artifact screen displays DML queries and results for each use case
- [ ] Backward compatibility with existing workflow state
- [ ] Unit tests for mapping logic
- [ ] Integration tests for end-to-end workflow

## Risk Assessment

**Low Risk:**
- Adding new fields to `WorkflowState` (backward compatible)
- Updating artifact transformation logic

**Medium Risk:**
- Modifying `DMLGenerationAgent` output format (requires prompt engineering)
- LLM consistency in generating structured output

**High Risk:**
- Breaking existing DML generation workflow
- Performance impact of structured output parsing

## Expected Benefits

1. **Enhanced User Experience**: Users can see exactly which DML operations correspond to each use case
2. **Better Debugging**: Easier to identify which use cases have issues with their DML
3. **Improved Artifact Quality**: More detailed and actionable artifact output
4. **Future Extensibility**: Foundation for per-usecase execution tracking and reporting

## Related Issues

This enhancement addresses the gap between use case generation and DML execution tracking, providing the foundation for more granular artifact reporting and better user visibility into the schema design process.
liam-hq/liam#2709.title: Implement DML-Usecase Mapping for Enhanced Artifact Display

liam-hq/liam#2710.body:
## Issue

Update `frontend/internal-packages/agent/src/chat/workflow/utils/withTimelineItemSync.test.ts` to use the new InMemoryRepository instead of manual repository mocks.

## Current State
This test file currently uses manual mocking of SchemaRepository (lines 28-42) with vi.fn() mocks for each method in the mockRepository object.

## Required Changes
1. Replace the manual mock repository creation (lines 28-42) with InMemoryRepository instantiation
2. Remove vi.fn() mocks for repository methods
3. Update test setup to use InMemoryRepository constructor options for initial data
4. Verify that timeline item creation actually works with the InMemoryRepository

## Benefits
- More reliable testing with actual repository behavior
- Reduced test brittleness
- Consistent with InMemoryRepository pattern established in PR #2689

## Reference Files
See `schemaDesignTool.test.ts` and `getToolConfigurable.test.ts` for examples of proper InMemoryRepository usage.
liam-hq/liam#2710.title: Replace repository mocks with InMemoryRepository in withTimelineItemSync.test.ts

## Issue

## Current State
This test file currently uses a `createMockRepositories()` function (lines 38-63) that creates vi.fn() mocks for all repository methods.

## Required Changes
1. Replace the `createMockRepositories()` function with InMemoryRepository instantiation
2. Remove vi.fn() mocks for repository methods like createValidationQuery, createValidationResults, etc.
3. Update test setup to use InMemoryRepository constructor options if needed
4. Ensure validation query and results functionality works with the InMemoryRepository

## Benefits
- More reliable testing with actual repository behavior
- Simplified test setup without complex mock functions
- Consistent with InMemoryRepository pattern established in PR #2689

The function groups timeline items and needs to be refactored to improve readability and maintainability. Consider:
- Breaking down the logic into smaller, focused helper functions
- Extracting complex conditions into well-named variables
- Simplifying the grouping logic

liam-hq/liam#3066.body: ## Issue - resolve: https://github.com/route06/liam-internal/issues/5393 ## Summary - Fix CodeRabbit configuration structure by moving `auto_review` to be properly nested under the `reviews` section before ``` auto_review: enabled: true drafts: true reviews: drafts: true ``` after ``` reviews: drafts: true auto_review: enabled: true drafts: true ``` docs https://docs.coderabbit.ai/reference/yaml-template ## Why is this change needed? The `auto_review` configuration was incorrectly placed as a top-level option instead of being nested under the `reviews` section, which could cause configuration parsing issues. ## Changes - Move `auto_review.enabled` and `auto_review.drafts` to be under `reviews.auto_review` 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) ## Testing When I created this pull request, coderabbit was running, so it looked good. ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **Chores** * Reorganized code review configuration by moving auto review settings into the reviews section, retaining existing options (enabled, drafts). * Ensures configuration consistency without altering functionality. * **Notes** * No user-facing changes or feature impacts.
liam-hq/liam#3066.title: fix(coderabbit): correct auto_review configuration nesting

liam-hq/liam#3153.body:
## Problem

The current test suites for agent graphs have two main issues:

1. **Missing README synchronization tests**: While `createGraph.test.ts` verifies that the generated Mermaid diagram matches what's documented in README.md, the subgraph tests (`createDbAgentGraph.test.ts`, `createQaAgentGraph.test.ts`) lack this critical validation. PM Agent (`createPmAgentGraph.ts`) doesn't even have a test file.

2. **Testing implementation details instead of observable behavior**: Current tests violate the "Test Observable Behavior" principle from our test-principles.md by checking internal node names, edge connections, and graph structure - all implementation details that consumers cannot observe.

## Current State

- **DB Agent** (`createDbAgentGraph.test.ts`): Has tests but lacks README sync test, contains unnecessary implementation detail tests
- **QA Agent** (`createQaAgentGraph.test.ts`): Has tests but lacks README sync test, contains unnecessary implementation detail tests  
- **PM Agent** (`createPmAgentGraph.test.ts`): No test file exists

## Proposed Solution

Following our test principles (test-principles.md), we should:

1. **Add README synchronization tests** for all agent subgraphs to ensure documentation stays accurate
2. **Remove implementation detail tests** that check node names, edge structures, and internal routing
3. **Keep only observable behavior tests** - specifically the Mermaid diagram generation

## Implementation Plan

For each agent graph test file:

### 1. Create/Update test files:
- Create `createPmAgentGraph.test.ts` 
- Update `createDbAgentGraph.test.ts`
- Update `createQaAgentGraph.test.ts`

### 2. Each test file should contain only:
```typescript
// 1. Test that generates correct Mermaid diagram
it('should create and return a compiled [Agent Name] subgraph', async () => {
  // Test Mermaid diagram generation
})

// 2. Test that README contains the same diagram
it('should have the same diagram in README.md as the generated graph', () => {
  // Verify README synchronization
})
```

### 3. Remove these implementation detail tests:
- Node structure verification tests
- Start node connection tests
- Edge routing tests
- Linear flow tests

## Benefits

- **Maintainability**: Tests won't break during refactoring of internal implementation
- **Documentation accuracy**: Ensures README stays in sync with actual code
- **Simplicity**: Fewer, more focused tests that test what matters
- **Follows best practices**: Aligns with Vladimir Khorikov's "observable behavior" principle

## References

- Test principles: `docs/test-principles.md`
- Example of good pattern: `frontend/internal-packages/agent/src/createGraph.test.ts` (lines 33-39)
liam-hq/liam#3153.title: Improve Agent Graph Tests - Remove Implementation Details and Add README Synchronization

liam-hq/liam#3154.body:
## Overview
The file `frontend/internal-packages/agent/src/chat/workflow/shared/langGraphUtils.ts` currently only contains a single constant (`DEFAULT_RECURSION_LIMIT`) but has a name suggesting it contains multiple utility functions for LangGraph. This naming mismatch makes the codebase less intuitive.

## Current State
- **File**: `frontend/internal-packages/agent/src/chat/workflow/shared/langGraphUtils.ts`
- **Content**: Single export `DEFAULT_RECURSION_LIMIT = 50` with extensive documentation
- **Name implication**: The suffix "Utils" suggests multiple utility functions or helpers

## Investigation Results
The file is being imported in 5 locations:
1. `frontend/internal-packages/agent/src/shared/workflowSetup.ts`
2. `frontend/internal-packages/agent/src/deepModeling.ts` 
3. `frontend/internal-packages/agent/src/db-agent/invokeDbAgent.ts`
4. `frontend/internal-packages/agent/scripts/executeDesignProcess.ts`
5. `frontend/internal-packages/agent/scripts/executeQaAgent.ts`

All imports only use the `DEFAULT_RECURSION_LIMIT` constant.

## Proposed Solution
Rename the file to one of the following more descriptive options:
1. **`workflowConstants.ts`** - Clearly indicates workflow-related constants
2. **`recursionLimits.ts`** - Specific to the current content
3. **`constants.ts`** - Generic but accurate for a file containing constants

## Action Items
1. Choose an appropriate filename from the suggestions above
2. Rename the file
3. Update all 5 import statements in the affected files
4. Consider if other workflow-related constants should be consolidated in this file

## Technical Considerations
- This is a straightforward refactoring with no functional changes
- All imports are direct and use named exports, making the update simple
- The change improves code readability and maintainability
- No risk to runtime behavior as it's purely a naming change

## Impact Analysis
- **Benefits**: Improved code clarity, better developer experience, more intuitive file organization
- **Risks**: None (simple rename operation)
- **Affected files**: 6 files total (1 renamed, 5 import updates)
liam-hq/liam#3154.title: Rename langGraphUtils.ts to better reflect its content

liam-hq/liam#3272.body:
## Overview The `useUniqueElementIds` biome rule needs to be enabled for the `@liam-hq/app` package to prevent duplicate IDs when React components are rendered multiple times. After enabling this rule, multiple lint errors have been detected that need to be fixed. ## Steps to Enable the Rule 1. Update `frontend/apps/app/biome.jsonc` with the following configuration: ```json { "extends": "//", "root": false, "linter": { "rules": { "nursery": { "useUniqueElementIds": "error" } } } } ``` This overrides the base configuration in `frontend/internal-packages/configs/biome.jsonc` where `useUniqueElementIds` is set to `"off"`. 2. Run lint to see the errors: ```bash cd frontend/apps/app && pnpm lint:biome ``` ## Lint Errors to Fix After enabling the rule, multiple `useUniqueElementIds` errors appear across various components in the app package. These errors occur where static string literals are used for ID attributes instead of dynamically generated unique IDs. ## Root Cause Various components throughout the app are using hardcoded IDs for HTML elements and SVG components. When these components are rendered multiple times on the same page, it creates duplicate IDs in the DOM, which: - Violates HTML specifications - Can cause accessibility issues - May lead to unexpected behavior with CSS or JavaScript selectors ## Required Fix Pattern All static IDs should be replaced with dynamically generated ones using React's `useId()` hook: ```tsx import { useId } from 'react' export const SomeComponent = () => { const elementId = useId() return (
{/* For HTML elements */} {/* For SVG elements */}
) } ``` ## Action Items - [ ] Enable the `useUniqueElementIds: "error"` rule in `frontend/apps/app/biome.jsonc` - [ ] Run `pnpm lint:biome` to identify all components with static ID violations - [ ] Fix all `useUniqueElementIds` errors by replacing static IDs with `useId()` generated IDs - [ ] Ensure any references to these IDs (like `htmlFor`, `clipPath`, etc.) are updated accordingly - [ ] Run `pnpm lint` to confirm all errors are resolved - [ ] Test affected components to ensure functionality remains intact ## Testing After implementing all fixes: 1. All lint errors should be resolved 2. Components should render correctly when used multiple times on the same page 3. All interactive elements (forms, SVGs, etc.) should maintain their functionality 4. No visual regression should occur ## Related - PR #3269 - Similar implementation for UI package components where this pattern was established - Part of the broader effort to enforce `useUniqueElementIds` across all packages to improve code quality
liam-hq/liam#3272.title: Enable useUniqueElementIds rule and fix all static ID violations (app package)

liam-hq/liam#3273.body:
## Overview The `useUniqueElementIds` biome rule needs to be enabled for the `@liam-hq/docs` package to prevent duplicate IDs when React components are rendered multiple times. After enabling this rule, lint errors have been detected that need to be fixed. ## Steps to Enable the Rule 1. Update `frontend/apps/docs/biome.jsonc` with the following configuration: ```json { "extends": "//", "root": false, "linter": { "rules": { "nursery": { "useUniqueElementIds": "error" }, "style": { "noRestrictedImports": { "options": { "paths": { "fumadocs-ui/components/tabs": "Use '@/components' instead.", "fumadocs-ui/components/callout": "Use '@/components' instead.", "fumadocs-ui/components/heading": "Use '@/components' instead.", "fumadocs-ui/components/banner": "Use '@/components' instead." } } } } } } } ``` Note: The `style.noRestrictedImports` configuration should be preserved from the existing configuration. This overrides the base configuration in `frontend/internal-packages/configs/biome.jsonc` where `useUniqueElementIds` is set to `"off"`. 2. Run lint to see the errors: ```bash cd frontend/apps/docs && pnpm lint:biome ``` ## Lint Errors to Fix After enabling the rule, `useUniqueElementIds` errors appear in components throughout the docs package. These errors occur where static string literals are used for ID attributes instead of dynamically generated unique IDs. ## Root Cause Components in the docs package are using hardcoded IDs for HTML elements. When React components with hardcoded IDs are rendered multiple times on the same page, it creates duplicate IDs in the DOM, which: - Violates HTML specifications - Can cause accessibility issues - May lead to unexpected behavior with CSS or JavaScript selectors ## Required Fix Pattern Replace static IDs with dynamically generated ones using React's `useId()` hook: ```tsx import { useId } from 'react' export const SomeComponent = () => { const elementId = useId() return (
) } ``` **Important Consideration**: Before applying fixes, verify if any IDs serve specific purposes: - Are they used for URL anchors (e.g., `#section-name`)? - Are they tracked in analytics systems? - Are they referenced by external systems? If an ID needs to be predictable, consider alternative approaches or document why that specific case should be excluded from the rule. ## Action Items - [ ] Enable the `useUniqueElementIds: "error"` rule in `frontend/apps/docs/biome.jsonc` - [ ] Run `pnpm lint:biome` to identify all components with static ID violations - [ ] For each violation, investigate if the ID serves a specific purpose - [ ] Fix all `useUniqueElementIds` errors by replacing static IDs with `useId()` generated IDs (where appropriate) - [ ] For IDs that must remain static, document the reason and consider rule exceptions - [ ] Run `pnpm lint` to ensure all resolvable errors are resolved - [ ] Test that all functionality remains intact ## Testing After implementing all fixes: 1. All resolvable lint errors should be resolved 2. All components should display correctly 3. Any functionality dependent on IDs should be verified (anchors, analytics, etc.) 4. No visual or functional regression should occur ## Related - Issue #3272 - Similar fix for app package - Issue #3274 - Similar fix for erd-core package - PR #3269 - Original implementation for UI package components - Part of the broader effort to enforce `useUniqueElementIds` across all packages
liam-hq/liam#3273.title: Enable useUniqueElementIds rule and fix all static ID violations (docs package)

liam-hq/liam#3279.body:
## Overview
The `noUnusedVariables` biome rule needs to be enabled for the `@liam-hq/app` package to maintain code quality by identifying and removing unused variables. After enabling this rule, lint errors have been detected that need to be fixed.

## Steps to Enable the Rule

1. Update `frontend/apps/app/biome.jsonc` with the following configuration:

```json
{
  "extends": "//",
  "root": false,
  "linter": {
    "rules": {
      "correctness": {
        "noUnusedVariables": "error"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

This overrides the base configuration in `frontend/internal-packages/configs/biome.jsonc` where `noUnusedVariables` is set to `"off"`.

2. Run lint to see the errors:

```bash
cd frontend/apps/app && pnpm lint:biome
```

## Lint Errors to Fix

After enabling the rule, `noUnusedVariables` errors appear in components throughout the app package. These errors occur where variables are declared but never used in the code.

## Root Cause
Various components contain variables that are:
- Declared but never referenced
- Destructured from objects but not used
- Function parameters that are unused
- Imported but not utilized

These unused variables:
- Create unnecessary noise in the code
- Can indicate incomplete implementation or refactoring oversight
- Reduce code readability and maintainability

## Required Fix Patterns

### 1. Remove Unused Variables
```tsx
// Before
const { children, className, unusedVar, ...rest } = props
const match = /language-(\w+)/.exec(className || '')

// After  
const { children, className, ...rest } = props
const match = /language-(\w+)/.exec(className || '')
```

### 2. Use Underscore Prefix for Intentionally Unused Variables
```tsx
// If the variable is needed for future functionality
const { children, className, _node, ...rest } = props
```

### 3. Remove Unused Imports
```tsx
// Before
import { useState, useEffect, unusedHook } from 'react'

// After
import { useState, useEffect } from 'react'
```

## Action Items
- [ ] Enable the `noUnusedVariables: "error"` rule in `frontend/apps/app/biome.jsonc`
- [ ] Run `pnpm lint:biome` to identify all unused variable violations
- [ ] For each violation, determine if the variable should be removed or used
- [ ] Remove genuinely unused variables
- [ ] Use underscore prefix for variables that are intentionally unused
- [ ] Add usage for variables that should be used but currently aren't
- [ ] Run `pnpm lint` to ensure all errors are resolved
- [ ] Verify components still function correctly after cleanup

## Testing
After implementing all fixes:
1. All lint errors should be resolved
2. All components should function exactly as before
3. Code should be cleaner and more maintainable
4. No functional regression should occur

## Related
- Issue #3280 - Similar fix for ui package
- Part of the broader effort to enforce `noUnusedVariables` across all packages to improve code quality
liam-hq/liam#3279.title: Enable noUnusedVariables rule and fix all unused variable violations (app package)

liam-hq/liam#3280.body:
## Overview The `noUnusedVariables` biome rule needs to be enabled for the `@liam-hq/ui` package to maintain code quality by identifying and removing unused variables. This is particularly important for the UI package as it's used throughout the entire application. After enabling this rule, lint errors have been detected that need to be fixed. ## Steps to Enable the Rule 1. Update `frontend/packages/ui/biome.jsonc` with the following configuration: ```json { "extends": "//", "root": false, "linter": { "rules": { "correctness": { "noUnusedVariables": "error" } } } } ``` This overrides the base configuration in `frontend/internal-packages/configs/biome.jsonc` where `noUnusedVariables` is set to `"off"`. 2. Run lint to see the errors: ```bash cd frontend/packages/ui && pnpm lint:biome ``` ## Lint Errors to Fix After enabling the rule, `noUnusedVariables` errors appear in UI components. These errors occur where variables are declared but never used in the code. ## Root Cause UI components contain variables that are: - Declared but never referenced - Destructured from props but not used - Computed values that aren't applied - Function parameters that are unused This is particularly problematic in the UI package because: - UI components should be clean and maintainable - Unused variables can confuse consumers of the components - The UI package affects all applications that depend on it ## Required Fix Patterns ### 1. Remove Unused Variables ```tsx // Before const SomeComponent = (props) => { const { children, className, unusedProp, ...rest } = props const unusedComputation = someCalculation() return
{children}
} // After const SomeComponent = (props) => { const { children, className, ...rest } = props return
{children}
} ``` ### 2. Use the Variable if Intended ```tsx // Before const SomeComponent = (props) => { const { children, className, ...rest } = props return
{children}
// className not used } // After const SomeComponent = (props) => { const { children, className, ...rest } = props return
{children}
// className now used } ``` ### 3. Use Underscore Prefix for Intentionally Unused Variables ```tsx // If the variable is needed for destructuring but not used const { children, className: _className, ...rest } = props ``` ## Action Items - [ ] Enable the `noUnusedVariables: "error"` rule in `frontend/packages/ui/biome.jsonc` - [ ] Run `pnpm lint:biome` to identify all unused variable violations - [ ] For each violation, determine the appropriate fix: - Remove if genuinely unused - Use if it should be applied to elements - Prefix with underscore if intentionally unused - [ ] Fix all `noUnusedVariables` errors - [ ] Run `pnpm lint` to ensure all errors are resolved - [ ] Test affected UI components in isolation - [ ] Test components in consuming applications (app, docs) - [ ] Update Storybook stories if component APIs change ## Testing After implementing all fixes: 1. All lint errors should be resolved 2. UI components should function exactly as before 3. All consuming applications should continue to work 4. Storybook should show no regressions 5. Component functionality should remain intact ## Related - Issue #3279 - Similar fix for app package - Critical for UI package quality as it affects all consuming applications - Part of the broader effort to enforce `noUnusedVariables` across all packages
liam-hq/liam#3280.title: Enable noUnusedVariables rule and fix all unused variable violations (ui package)
